Parasara mentions 16 Vargas. But generally six or seven
Vargas only are made use of in astrology. The Horapradipa
has given a clear analysis of the functions of the seven Vargas
mentioned above. Everything concerning the body is to be
judged from the Lagna as a whole; things pertaining to wealth
and property from Hora, the result o/Karma from the Decanate,
brothers and sisters from Saptarhsa, issue from Navamsa,
marriage from Dvadasamsa and lastly death from Trirhsarhsa.
At present we are not concerned with the Vargas other than the
Drekkana. The word Drekkana like Tavuru is not a Sanskrit
one. It must be a loan word from Greek where it must have
meant an arc subtending an angle of 10 degrees at the centre of a
circle. Hence the word Drekkana or decanate corresponds to
Dasa in Sanskrit and to decem in Latin. Greek astrologers
attached great importance to this decanate possibly on account
of the special significance of the number ten. In India too we
